channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula falava sobre Ozempic ao recomendar andar a pé, e não sobre combustível
item.description: President Lula (PT) promoted walking and physical activity while discussing the use of Ozempic for diabetes and obesity treatment, not suggesting people walk due to high fuel costs. Misleading posts edit his remarks to omit context, falsely claiming he linked the recommendation to rising gasoline prices. Lula stressed that the drug should not be a 'gift' for those who avoid exercise or unhealthy eating, emphasizing that physical activity and medical guidance are necessary.

item.title: Veja quem são os vices que assumem com a saída de Paes, João Campos e Zema
item.description: Mayors Eduardo Paes (PSD) of Rio de Janeiro and João Campos (PSB) of Recife are expected to resign from their positions in the coming days to run for state governor in Rio and Pernambuco in October 2026. Their respective vice mayors will assume the mayoral offices until the end of the current terms.

item.title: Kassab escolhe Ratinho para disputar Presidência pelo PSD, diz conselheiro
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab is set to nominate Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. as the party's presidential candidate for 2026, following consultation with a veteran council led by Jorge Bornhausen. The decision was unanimous among tested PSD governors, citing Ratinho’s center-right profile and broad electoral appeal across social strata. Official confirmation is expected between March 25 and 27, before the deadline for candidates to resign from current offices.

item.title: Os taxistas de Cármen Lúcia tinham razão: desconfiança no STF rompe a polarização pela primeira vez
item.description: Trust in the Supreme Federal Court (STF) dropped to 34% while distrust rose to 60%, breaking for the first time the barrier of political polarization, according to AtlasIntel. Even among Lula voters, one in four now distrusts the Court, showing the legitimacy crisis transcends ideological lines. Minister Cármen Lúcia noted that, unlike in 2015, even taxi drivers now criticize the STF, reflecting a broad erosion of confidence following the Master case revelations.

item.title: Zema deixa governo de MG no domingo, e Mateus Simões toma posse
item.description: Romeu Zema (Novo) resigns as governor of Minas Gerais this Sunday, with his vice, Mateus Simões (PSD), set to assume office at 10 a.m. in the ALMG. The ceremony will be led by state deputy Tadeu Leite (MDB), followed by a formal handover at the Palácio da Liberdade. Zema is considered a potential presidential candidate for Novo, while Simões has suggested that a partnership with Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) could unify the right in Minas and boost his electoral prospects in October.

item.title: Luciano Hang coloca peruca loira para criticar Erika Hilton
item.description: Luciano Hang, owner of Havan, posted a video on X wearing a blonde wig and women's clothing to criticize Erika Hilton's (Psol-SP) election as president of the Women's Rights Defense Commission, questioning her legitimacy and citing an 84% disagreement rate. He compared his act to that of Deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG), who in 2023 wore a wig during International Women's Day and was later ordered to pay R$200,000 in collective moral damages. Governists defended Hilton, while Hang and Ferreira's allies reinforced transphobic criticisms of her leadership.

item.title: EUA investigam Petro por suposta ligação com traficantes de drogas
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro is under criminal investigation by two U.S. federal prosecutors’ offices in New York over alleged ties to drug traffickers and possible solicitation of illegal donations during his presidential campaign. The investigations, led by narcotics specialists and involving the DEA, are in early stages and have not yet led to charges. While there is no evidence the White House initiated the probes, Donald Trump has used the case to pressure Colombia and potentially influence the country’s May 2026 presidential election.

item.title: O calendário dos sonhos do relator do PL de regulamentação de trabalho por apps
item.description: The rapporteur of the bill regulating app-based work, Deputy Augusto Coutinho (Republicans), set a timeline to finalize the text by late March and vote it in committee or plenary by early April. The main disagreement is the minimum payment per delivery: R$ 8.50 proposed by the rapporteur versus R$ 10 plus R$ 2.50 per additional kilometer advocated by the government. Despite concerns about negative electoral impact in 2026, the government still views the proposal as a priority.

item.title: Crusoé: De faccionados para terroristas
item.description: The Brazilian federal government, through Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira, opposes the U.S. proposal to designate the First Capital Command (PCC) and the Red Command (CV) as Foreign Terrorist Organizations (FTOs). Vieira argues that such a move could harm international cooperation against organized crime without addressing its root causes. The potential designation is noted as a factor that could influence the 2026 presidential election landscape.

item.title: Entra em vigor lei que amplia garantias para financiamentos do Pronaf
item.description: Law 13.356/26, signed by President Lula, authorizes the Guarantee Fund for Operations (FGO) to guarantee Pronaf credit operations, unlocking up to R$500 million in uncommitted funds to expand credit for family farming. The measure eliminates the pecuniary commission, reduces risk for banks, and facilitates access to loans for production costs, equipment, and property investment — especially for small producers lacking traditional collateral.

item.title: Em novo aceno a policiais no ano eleitoral, Tarcísio pagará bônus por desempenho
item.description: The São Paulo government announced a R$ 609 million performance bonus (BR) for police officers for 2025, to be paid in April. Governor Tarcísio de Freitas said the payment recognizes police dedication and performance, tied to SSP-defined indicators, and strengthens public safety. The move comes amid pressure for wage increases, as the governor also submitted four bills to the state legislature proposing around a 10% salary raise for civil and military police.

item.title: Haddad só venceu uma das 4 eleições que concorreu
item.description: Fernando Haddad is running for his fifth election in 2026 as the official pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo. Despite winning only one of his four previous electoral races (São Paulo mayor in 2012), he aims to regain political influence in Brazil’s most populous state. His candidacy is seen as a strategic first step to organize opposition against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro in 2026 and strengthen the PT’s foundation for Lula’s potential fourth term.

item.title: Lula escala ministros e nomes históricos do PT para coordenação da campanha à reeleição
item.description: President Lula (PT) is assembling his 2026 re-election campaign team, with general coordination led by PT national president Edinho Silva and Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira. The team includes ministers such as Wellington Dias, Guilherme Boulos, and Carlos Fávaro, alongside historic PT figures like Paulo Okamotto, Gilberto Carvalho, and José Sergio Gabrielli, each assigned specific roles in social movement outreach, agribusiness dialogue, gubernatorial and mayoral engagement, and agenda and government plan formulation.

item.title: Misoginia será a próxima prioridade regulatória no ecossistema digital
item.description: The Lula government is preparing new digital regulations to combat online misogyny, inspired by the STF's ruling on Article 19 of the Marco Civil da Internet. Drafts are under discussion between Secom and the Ministry of Justice, with one or two decrees possible, though no release date has been set. The initiative is gaining momentum in Congress, with Bill PL 2/2026 introduced as a response to the rise of 'red pill' ideology among young men.

item.title: Aécio e Temer se reúnem em SP e falam em 'alternativa' contra polarização
item.description: Aécio Neves, president of the PSDB, met with Michel Temer in São Paulo to discuss an 'alternative' to political polarization in Brazil. The meeting occurs as the party seeks to regain relevance during the 2026 party window, attracting lawmakers and attempting to rebuild its electoral base, recalling the nearly 50% of votes Aécio received in 2014.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula visita Refinaria Gabriel Passos em Minas Gerais
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva visits the Gabriel Passos Refinery in Betim, Minas Gerais, where he accompanies Petrobras' investment announcement and the inauguration of the state-owned company's first solar power plant. The plant, featuring 20,000 solar panels across 20 hectares with a R$63 million investment, began operations in December 2025 and aims to replace natural gas combustion with clean energy, a model already replicated at the Abreu e Lima and Paulínia refineries.

item.title: Polícia indicia mulher que xingou deputado do PL de "verme" e "lixo"
item.description: The Federal Legislative Police indicted Roberta da Horta for defamation and injury with aggravating circumstances after she insulted federal deputy André Fernandes (PL-CE) as a 'worm', 'trash', and other offensive terms at Fortaleza airport in December 2025, also mentioning Jair and Michelle Bolsonaro. The PLF requested precautionary measures prohibiting physical and indirect contact, including on social media, to protect the legislator and prevent recurrence. The accused remained silent during deposition and sought legal aid, claiming 'fighting is not a crime'.

item.title: Brasil resiste à pressão dos EUA pelas terras raras, diz “NYT”
item.description: Brazil has resisted U.S. pressure to sign a bilateral agreement for critical minerals exploration, including rare earths, lithium, and cobalt, despite being invited to a forum in São Paulo. The Lula government aims to retain control over its resources and build a complete domestic value chain, avoiding raw material exports, while the U.S. seeks purchase priority in exchange for investment.

item.title: Lei Felca: conheça o regulamento do ECA Digital
item.description: The Federal Government enacted Law No. 15.211/2025, known as the Felca Law or Digital ECA, establishing digital protection rules for children and adolescents. Effective since March 17, the law mandates safety measures for digital platforms, including strict age verification and monitoring tools for guardians. President Lula signed three related decrees: the ECA Digital regulation, the creation of the National Center for Child and Adolescent Protection linked to the Federal Police, and the structuring of the ANPD to oversee compliance.

item.title: Boulos vai deixar o PSOL e se filiar ao PT; objetivo é substituir Lula em 2030
item.description: Guilherme Boulos, Minister of the General Secretariat of the Presidency and federal deputy, announced his departure from PSOL and affiliation with the PT, as reported by O Globo and confirmed by O Antagonista. The move aims to position him as a potential successor to Lula in 2030. Boulos communicated the decision to the national executive of PSOL during the night.

item.title: Ausência de Michelle provoca estrago em área do PL que era vitrine eleitoral
item.description: The absence of Michelle Bolsonaro from the PL Mulher agenda, which she leads, has caused concern within the party, as the women's wing has halted affiliation events and she refuses to delegate her duties, maintaining only contact with state presidents. Her inactivity since November last year, when Jair Bolsonaro was imprisoned, is seen as a strategic setback, as her nationwide tours would serve as a counterpoint to the Lula government's women's rights campaigns and keep the Bolsonaro family active in the party's grassroots.

item.title: De volta ao bolsonarismo, Moro transforma o Paraná em base para tentar barrar Lula outra vez
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro plans to switch from União Brasil to the PL to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, strengthening his alliance with Bolsonarism and supporting Flávio Bolsonaro’s presidential bid. His return to the PL reflects an attempt to regain political relevance as a local leader of the far-right, after being sidelined in the Senate. The move also deepens the rift between the PL and Governor Ratinho Junior, who maintains independent presidential ambitions.

item.title: Hugo Souza, do Corinthians, é convocado por Ancelotti; Alisson é cortado da lista
item.description: Corinthians goalkeeper Hugo Souza has been called up to the Brazil national team for friendlies against France and Croatia in the United States, replacing injured Alisson. He was added to Carlo Ancelotti's squad after being initially left out. Souza stood out in Corinthians' win over Chapecoense, making at least three key saves in the second half.

item.title: Republicano, Chuck Norris convocou evangélicos a votarem contra Obama
item.description: Chuck Norris, action film star and public advocate of Republican causes, died at age 86 in 2026. He was known for supporting Republican candidates, particularly through a 2012 video with his wife, Gena O'Kelley, urging evangelicals not to vote for Barack Obama, claiming his administration was leading the U.S. toward socialism.

item.title: Haddad defende Alckmin como vice de Lula em 2026
item.description: Former Finance Minister Fernando Haddad, the PT's pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo, defended Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) remaining as Lula's vice-presidential running mate in the 2026 re-election bid, calling the duo 'natural' and victorious. Haddad said he will consult Alckmin on choosing his own running mate for the state race against Tarcísio de Freitas, acknowledging challenges in penetrating the interior of São Paulo while emphasizing the left's best result in the state in 2022.

item.title: Haddad diz que não privatizaria Sabesp, mas que respeita o contrato
item.description: Fernando Haddad stated that, although he would not have privatized Sabesp, he respects the contracts made by previous administrations and believes reversing privatizations is complex and not always economically viable. He emphasized that PT governments uphold contractual commitments, even when they disagree with past policies, to avoid greater economic harm.

item.title: Douglas Ruas, Felipe Curi e outros 9 secretários deixam o governo do Rio para disputar as eleições
item.description: Eleven secretaries from the Rio de Janeiro state government, including state deputy Douglas Ruas and Public Security Secretary Felipe Curi, resigned to run in the October elections. Ruas is Flávio Bolsonaro's pick for state governor, while Curi aims for a federal deputy seat. The departures are part of a political strategy by Governor Cláudio Castro and Flávio Bolsonaro to support the presidential project of former president Jair Bolsonaro in Rio.

item.title: Zema em chapa de Flávio unificaria direita em Minas, mas discussão é nacional, diz vice-governador
item.description: Vice-governor Mateus Simões (PSD) stated that Romeu Zema (Novo) joining Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL) presidential ticket in 2026 could unite the right in Minas Gerais and support his own re-election bid. He noted, however, that alliance discussions are national in scope and not confined to the state.

item.title: Arthur Lira lança pré-candidatura ao Senado em evento em Maceió
item.description: Federal Deputy Arthur Lira (PP-AL), former president of the Chamber of Deputados, officially launched his pre-candidacy for the Senate from Alagoas in the 2026 elections at an event in Maceió, attended by state-level leaders.

item.title: 'Papel de um bom juiz não é ser estrela', diz Mendonça
item.description: STF Minister André Mendonça stated that the role of a good judge is not to be a star, but to decide correctly for the right reasons. He emphasized discretion and austerity in judicial proceedings, noting his role as rapporteur in the Banco Master investigations and the INSS inquiry involving a son of President Lula. His comments come amid tensions with Alexandre de Moraes and rising political prominence ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Haddad atribui derrota em 2022 a “dificuldades” no interior de SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad attributed his 2022 defeat in São Paulo to difficulties in the state's interior, where he received only 35% of the vote compared to 55% in the metropolitan region. He noted that, despite the loss, his 44.73% share represented the best historical performance by the progressive field in São Paulo. Haddad emphasized the importance of dialogue with sectors unsympathetic to the left to advance public policies in health and education.

item.title: Quais brasileiros se deram bem no sorteio da Libertadores – e quem se deu mal
item.description: In the 2026 Libertadores draw, Flamengo, Fluminense, Corinthians and Palmeiras landed in favorable groups with accessible paths to qualification. Meanwhile, Mirassol and Cruzeiro faced tough draws: Mirassol will contend with extreme altitude and strong opponents like LDU and Lanús, while Cruzeiro was placed in a 'group of death' featuring Boca Juniors, Barcelona de Guayaquil and Universidad Católica.

item.title: A condição do PL de Valdemar para apoiar o fim da escala 6×1
item.description: PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto stated the party could support ending the 6×1 work schedule only if compensations are provided to employers, such as reduced payroll taxes. The shift in stance followed a meeting with Flávio Bolsonaro and campaign strategist Duda Lima, aiming to appease both business interests and factory workers. Valdemar warned that without such measures, the PL would mobilize workers in opposition to the proposal.

item.title: Arthur Lira lança pré-candidatura ao Senado Federal por AL durante evento em Maceió
item.description: Federal deputy Arthur Lira (PP) launched his pre-candidacy for the Federal Senate from Alagoas during an event in Maceió, emphasizing his tenure as former Chamber president and pledging to honor his father’s legacy, former senator Benedito de Lira. He is contesting one of the state’s two Senate seats in 2026 and noted that his 18-year-old son, Álvaro Lira, is expected to run for federal deputy.

item.title: Haddad defende Alckmin como vice de Lula e diz ser cedo para definir sucessão em 2030
item.description: Fernando Haddad defended the maintenance of Geraldo Alckmin as vice president on Lula's 2026 ticket, stating the PT is comfortable with the 2022 choice and that Alckmin is key to broadening support in São Paulo's interior. He also downplayed the idea of being Lula's political successor in 2030, saying it is too early to discuss and that his current focus is on the São Paulo gubernatorial pre-candidacy.

item.title: A crítica de Lula sobre a política e os partidos brasileiros
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva strongly criticized Brazilian politics, stating it has 'rotted' due to widespread promiscuity among parties and calling for positive examples to restore credibility. He emphasized his government's role in uncovering corruption scandals, including those related to pensions and the Master Bank, which he attributed to the Bolsonaro administration and Roberto Campos Neto. Despite the electoral tone, Lula's speech warned of the dangers of an 'anti-system' climate, as highlighted by PT leader Edinho Silva, which could fuel the rise of outsiders and extremist candidacies.

item.title: Haddad diz que Tebet tem mais raízes em SP que Tarcísio e afirma não rejeitar apoio de conservadores
item.description: Former Finance Minister Fernando Haddad (PT) defended Simone Tebet (MDB) after she was called a 'foreigner' by allies of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos), stating she has deeper roots in São Paulo than the governor. Haddad confirmed Tebet will change her electoral domicile to São Paulo and run for Senate on his ticket. He also said he does not rule out support from conservative parties, as long as principles and values are upheld, citing the decisive PP backing in his 2012 São Paulo mayoral campaign.

item.title: As ações da força-tarefa do TRE do Rio para blindar eleições do crime organizado
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Regional Electoral Court (TRE-RJ) has formed a task force to counter organized crime infiltration in the 2026 elections, including identifying and replacing polling stations in high-risk areas and sharing intelligence on candidates with possible criminal ties. Led by Judge Claudio de Mello Tavares, the initiative aims to prevent voter coercion, illegal campaign financing, and candidacies linked to criminal factions, integrating security agencies and the Regional Electoral Prosecutor’s Office.

item.title: Haddad defende Alckmin vice de Lula, reedita 2022 e diz que Tebet tem mais raiz que Tarcísio em SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad, former Finance Minister and pre-candidate for Governor of São Paulo, defended Geraldo Alckmin remaining as Lula’s vice-presidential running mate in 2026, arguing the campaign should replicate the successful 2022 strategy. He criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for lacking familiarity with the state, stating that Simone Tebet has deeper roots in São Paulo than the current governor.

item.title: 'Tarcísio não tem nenhuma familiaridade até hoje com SP', diz Haddad em 1ª entrevista como pré-candidato a governador
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) officially launched his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo in 2026, rebutting criticisms from Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos), whom he accused of having 'no familiarity whatsoever with São Paulo'. Haddad also defended Simone Tebet's (MDB) Senate bid, stating she has deeper roots in the state than Tarcísio, and emphasized his own political fighting spirit since 2016.

item.title: No STF, Zanin vota para manter privatização da Sabesp; Fux suspende julgamento
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin, the rapporteur of the case, voted to deny the motions seeking to suspend the São Paulo law authorizing the privatization of Sabesp, citing procedural grounds without addressing the merits. Justice Luiz Fux then requested a review, transferring the decision to the physical plenary with no set date. The issue had been discussed in meetings between Governor Tarcísio de Freitas and several justices on Wednesday.

item.title: Quem é Eduardo Cavaliere, advogado que assume como prefeito mais jovem da história do Rio
item.description: Eduardo Cavaliere, 31, becomes the youngest mayor in Rio de Janeiro's history after Eduardo Paes (PSD) steps down to run for state governor. A lawyer and close aide to Paes, Cavaliere previously led the Municipal Secretariat of Environment and the Chief of Staff office, and was elected state deputy in 2022 before returning to City Hall. His appointment reflects the continuity of Paes’ political project within the PSD.

item.title: Lula escala ministro para participar da coordenação de campanha
item.description: Social Development Minister Wellington Dias has been appointed to join the coordination of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's (PT) re-election campaign. He will contribute to political articulation with governors, mayors and leaders, particularly in the Northeast. Dias, a former governor of Piauí and licensed senator, will use accumulated vacation time to focus on campaign activities.

item.title: Haddad: Tarcísio não cumpriu promessa de combate à violência contra a mulher
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for failing to fulfill his promise to prioritize combating violence against women, made during a debate. Haddad was confirmed as the PT’s pre-candidate for the São Paulo governorship in 2026, as part of a strategy to strengthen opposition to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. The state is pivotal in the presidential race due to its electorate of 34.4 million voters.

item.title: Doechii curte balada em São Paulo um dia antes de seu show no Lollapalooza
item.description: Rapper Doechii was spotted enjoying a nightclub in downtown São Paulo, a day before her Lollapalooza 2026 performance. She was seen dancing and taking photos with fans at the Martinelli Building, showing warmth and connection with her audience. Doechii, a Grammy winner for Best Rap Album, is regarded as one of the leading voices in female rap in the U.S. and is set to perform at the festival this Friday.

item.title: Crusoé: Felca defende a Lei Felca (mais ou menos)
item.description: Influencer Felipe Bressanim, known as Felca, posted a video defending the 'Lei Felca' (Digital ECA), which caused online confusion and prompted the Lula government to delay signing the decree. However, he stated his support is partial or conditional, indicating he backs the law's intent but has concerns about its execution.

item.title: A decisão de Tereza Cristina sobre a chapa com Flávio Bolsonaro ao Planalto
item.description: Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) declined the invitation to be the running mate on Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) presidential ticket, choosing to remain in the Senate. She was the preferred choice of PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto for vice president due to her gender, background as former Agriculture Minister, and appeal to the União-PP federation and agribusiness sector. The PL is now seeking another woman to fill the vice-presidential slot.

item.title: Meio-Dia em Brasília: o que Vorcaro vai falar na delação premiada
item.description: The Meio-Dia em Brasília program this Friday, the 20th, will cover the initial negotiations for the plea bargain of banker Daniel Vorcaro, former controller of Banco Master, and discuss the tribute made by Minister Gilmar Mendes to Alexandre de Moraes at the STF, in addition to mentioning an unspecified pre-candidacy.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula ainda tenta empurrar Alckmin para o Senado
item.description: President Lula (PT) continues to try to convince Vice President Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) to run for the Senate from São Paulo in 2026, despite saying the vice-presidential spot remains open for him. During an event at the ABC Metalworkers Union, Lula said he would be very happy to have the former São Paulo governor as a Senate candidate.

item.title: Governo edita MPs para baratear diesel e garantir piso do frete
item.description: The government issued Provisional Measures 1.344/2026 and 1.343/2026 to curb rising diesel prices and protect the national freight floor, responding to oil price volatility from the Middle East war. MP 1.344/2026 allocates up to R$10 billion in subsidies for road-use diesel until December 31, 2026, and increases export tariffs on crude oil (12%) and diesel (50%) to boost domestic supply. MP 1.343/2026 introduces penalties, including suspension of the RNTRC, for employers paying below the minimum freight rate, aiming to prevent trucker strikes.
